title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Authorization, Ethics, Privacy, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Source Authorization
summary: A team says “scrape this public site” without a site owner, permitted purpose, data policy, stop contact, accessibility outcome, or proof that the result helps anyone. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the source authorization increment in authorized stakeholder and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.
Define the stakeholder decision, users, page population, evidence, success, failure, review cadence, and observable value before choosing crawler technology.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-outcomes-authorization-evidence-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-outcomes-authorization-evidence-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_outcomes_authorization_evidence_3(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, minimized_fields: int = 6, retained_fields: int = 6, transfer_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authorization-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if minimized_fields < 1 or retained_fields != minimized_fields:
        return False, retained_fields, "data-minimization"
    if not transfer_gates_named:
        return False, retained_fields, "evidence-overclaim"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "authorized-crawl-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Build Source Authorization evidence · Theory Source Authorization
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-source-authorization-h64xzi" that uses case 4098 to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Build Source Authorization evidence · Theory Source Authorization.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Source Authorization into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Build Source Authorization evidence · Theory Source Authorization, keep Source Authorization connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-source-authorization-h64xzi
# Competency: scraper-source-authorization.
# Case 4098: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_source_authorization_h64xzi(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, minimized_fields: int = 6, retained_fields: int = 6, transfer_gates_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_h64xzi = "h64xzi-izx46h-4098"
    assert evidence_variant_h64xzi
    evidence_axes_h64xzi = (
        "authorizationh64xzi",
        "url4098",
        "responseizx46h",
        "treeh64xzi4098",
        "frontier4098h64xzi",
        "reportizx46h4098",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_h64xzi)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authorization-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if minimized_fields < 1 or retained_fields != minimized_fields:
        return False, retained_fields, "data-minimization"
    if not transfer_gates_named:
        return False, retained_fields, "evidence-overclaim"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "authorized-crawl-charter"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Source Authorization model · Theory Source Authorization
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Source Authorization: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 9555 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the crawl charter on authorization, stakeholder value, minimized data, and honest evidence limits; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Source Authorization model · Theory Source Authorization.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Source Authorization model · Theory Source Authorization, keep Source Authorization connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record who controls the target, what access is authorized, which identities and paths are permitted, and who can pause or revoke the crawl.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-source-authorization-h64xzi(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_source_authorization_h64xzi\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not authorized)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?acceptance_cases < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?minimized_fields < 1 or retained_fields != minimized_fields)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not transfer_gates_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
